response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with broad polyglot experience and current Staff-level title at re:cinq, including hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling — directly relevant to the agentic-coding mindset this role requires. However, the resume does not list Python in the skills section, and the must-have 'deep expertise in distributed systems and high-throughput architectures' is only partially evidenced (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby millions-of-req/sec work was primarily in DLang, several years ago). Strong craftsmanship signals (TDD, workshops, OSS) but limited explicit evidence of technical-strategy scope and stakeholder coordination at staff level.
strengths: Current Staff role with hands-on MCP/agentic tooling experience matching the AI-native mindset
Prior high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/sec)
Strong software-craftsmanship track record: TDD, CI/CD, OSS maintainership, public talks and workshops
concerns: Python not listed in skills — must-have for this role; primary languages are TS/Node and DLang
Limited explicit evidence of technical strategy, stakeholder management, and on-call/operational ownership at scale
High-throughput distributed-systems experience is several years old and in DLang rather than the role's target stack
